# HOHO Preparse Comparison

Generated: 2026-07-13T03:03:31.583Z
Input rows: 30
Git HEAD used for HEAD/preview local runner: b061ae087a0fa3cac114f20e75080a39487d84d2

## Setups

- Current deployed production, flag off: live production `/api/openai` and `/api/validate` at `https://tree-service-web-app.vercel.app` with no per-request preparse toggle.
- Current deployed production, preparse proxy: HEAD pre-normalizer output is sent as `customer_text` to live production `/api/openai`, then validated against the original raw note. This approximates whether production AI might benefit from preparse, but it is not identical to enabling the production env flag.
- HEAD/preview code, pre-normalizers on: git HEAD code is run locally with `ENABLE_TD1_PRE_NORMALIZERS=true`. This represents the preview branch code if the preview deployment had that env flag enabled.

## How To Test Production Exactly

Production cannot be toggled per request because the route checks `process.env.ENABLE_TD1_PRE_NORMALIZERS === "true"`.
An exact production-benefit test requires a production-equivalent deployment or preview that uses the current production code plus `ENABLE_TD1_PRE_NORMALIZERS=true`.
The proxy setup in this report is a practical signal, not a perfect substitute.

Input text:

```text

[63-70]\n* Option boundary clues:\n  - C (bare_option_label) [8-9]\n  - option a: (explicit_option_label) [106-115]\n  - a (bare_option_label) [113-114]\n  - option b: (explicit_option_label) [147-156]\n  - b (bare_option_label) [154-155]\n* Option-price pairings:\n  - price_1 Option A: no description -> $5,500 (medium) [113-121]\n  - price_2 Option B: no description -> $6,350 (medium) [154-162]\n* Stump/add-on clues:\n  - plus (add_on_boundary) [90-94]\n  - haul (haul_cleanup_or_debris) [170-174]\n  - logs (haul_cleanup_or_debris) [175-179]\n  - grind stump (stump_or_grinding) [180-191]\n  - clean up (haul_cleanup_or_debris) [192-200]\n* Add-on price interpretations:\n  - add_on_price_1_price_2: $5,500 + $6,350 -> $6,350 (bundled_total; medium) [116-162]\n* Price/scope ambiguity warnings:\n  - Possible add-on price needs confirmation before automatic bundled-option arithmetic. [116-162]\n* Safety/access/scope warnings:\n  - wire (Raw note contains safety, access, damage, or scope language that should be reviewed by Tree Dude.) [95-99]\n"
